reasons: Diggie reduces Vexana's teamfight control value.
Time Journey can help allies survive or reset after Vexana's CC setup.

proof.summary: Vexana wants to start fights with control and delayed AoE follow-up, while Diggie can cleanse and protect nearby allies during the control window.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves ultimate for Vexana's real CC combo.
Allies stay close enough to receive protection.
failureCases: Vexana baits Diggie ultimate with minor control.
Diggie is caught before the main combo.

reasons: Lancelot can dodge delayed Vexana skill timing.
He can punish Vexana's immobility after her control skills are used.

proof.summary: Vexana relies on delayed area control, while Lancelot can use invulnerable or untargetable windows to dodge and then burst her.
worksBestWhen: Lancelot waits for Deathly Grasp or Cursed Blast to be committed.
failureCases: Lancelot dives into Eternal Guard and enemy follow-up.
Vexana holds CC until Lancelot's immunity ends.

reasons: Kagura can reposition away from Vexana's delayed AoE and remove debuffs.
Her umbrella poke lets her pressure Vexana without standing in predictable zones.

proof.summary: Vexana wants to trap enemies in control zones, while Kagura can cleanse, dash, and reposition around delayed skill timing.
worksBestWhen: Kagura keeps mobility for Deathly Grasp or Cursed Blast timing.
Kagura pokes from angles instead of walking straight into Vexana's zone.
failureCases: Kagura uses her escape too early.
Vexana’s frontline forces Kagura into a narrow path.

reasons: Natalia can punish Vexana before Vexana sets up a front-to-back fight.
Backline pressure forces Vexana to spend skills defensively.

proof.summary: Vexana is strongest when casting safely behind her frontline, while Natalia threatens her isolated rotations and backline position.
worksBestWhen: Natalia attacks from fog before Vexana groups.
Vexana's control skills are on cooldown or aimed elsewhere.
failureCases: Vexana stays protected with vision and peel.
Natalia enters after Eternal Guard and CC are already available.

reasons: Saber can remove or force out Vexana before she gets full teamfight value.
Targeted burst is useful when Vexana is exposed after casting.

proof.summary: Vexana wants enough time to layer CC, delayed burst, and summon pressure, while Saber can force a fast single-target pickoff.
worksBestWhen: Saber catches Vexana outside tank cover.
Saber waits until Vexana's main CC is unavailable.
failureCases: Saber dives into multiple enemies without follow-up.
